prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "蚌式开合" (clamshell) exercise, three-quarter view from the front. Person posture: Athletic person doing a clamshell exercise: lying on the right side on the ground; both knees bent to about 45 degrees with feet stacked and touching each other; hips stacked vertically; the top knee (left) is rotated open and clearly lifted away from the bottom knee while the feet remain in contact, like a clamshell opening; right arm tucked under the head, left hand resting on the hip; spine neutral; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus medius of the top hip. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus (upper fibers) and deep external hip rotators. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius of the top hip; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "髋外旋肌群" pointing to the deep external hip rotators. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
